commit.message:
fix: decouple --force flag — add --rebuild to rebuild parquets from cache

Session 18 discovered that --force controls both the parquet existence
check and the per-record API cache check. This makes it impossible to
regenerate a parquet from cached raw files without re-fetching from
the API — a problem when extraction times out partway through.

--rebuild bypasses the parquet check while preserving per-record cache,
enabling resume-from-cache without redundant API calls.

Affected: extract_major_holders, extract_bondholder_register,
extract_revenue_schedule, extract_seibro_repricing, extract_dart,
pipeline.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.35.0 — Уведомления при свёрнутом окне (backgroundThrottling=false)

Electron по умолчанию замораживает JS в renderer/WebView при скрытии окна.
MutationObserver, Notification hooks и IPC останавливались — уведомления
не приходили, пока окно не развернуть.

Фикс: backgroundThrottling=false в BrowserWindow webPreferences,
runtime override на webContents, и webpreferences="backgroundThrottling=no"
на каждом <webview>.

commit.message:
Fix tangency portfolio legend icon not visible

Use borderColor for the legend and pointBorderColor for the on-chart
star so the legend icon is red instead of invisible white.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix table parser: handle pagination responses without <tbody> wrapper

Pagination AJAX responses from PrimeFaces return raw <tr> elements
directly in CDATA without a <tbody> wrapper. The _extract_table()
method required <tbody> to find rows, so it returned empty results
for every pagination page despite the server correctly returning data.

Now falls back to extracting <tr> rows directly from the HTML when
no <tbody> is present.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: explicit workspace context via URL path parameter (#68)

* refactor: rename TenantWorkspace.tenant_id to external_tenant_id

Avoids shadowing Django's auto-generated tenant_id FK column on the
OneToOneField. Updates all 10 callsites across agent tools and recipe runner.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add failing tests for path-scoped tenant workspace resolution

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: scope knowledge, recipe, artifact, and data-dictionary URLs under /<resource>/<tenant_id>/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: update frontend slices to use tenant-scoped resource URLs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: clarify last_selected_at is UX ordering only, not workspace resolution

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update share and query-data tests for tenant-scoped URLs

Add tenant_id to all URL reversals in test_share_api.py and
test_artifact_query_data.py. Add creator TenantMembership in share API
test setup and a membership fixture in query data tests.

Also fix ArtifactQueryDataView to use select_related("tenant") on the
membership aget, avoiding a synchronous lazy FK load in async context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: extract shared resolve_workspace utility

Create apps/projects/workspace_resolver.py with a single
resolve_workspace(request, tenant_id) function. All four resource apps
(knowledge, recipes, projects, artifacts) now share the same membership
lookup + workspace resolution logic.

Also:
- Fix apps/projects/api/views.py: standardise to 2-tuple return, change
  _resolve_tenant_schema to take tenant directly (callers use workspace.tenant)
- Fix apps/artifacts/views.py: add module-level TenantMembership/TenantWorkspace
  imports, replace 6 inline blocks with _resolve_workspace/_aresolve_workspace
  helpers, remove unused SANDBOX_CSP constant
- Fix apps/artifacts/api/views.py: use shared resolver in
  ArtifactSharePermissionMixin.get_artifact

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: drop superuser bypass in share permission, add projects API tests

- Remove is_superuser shortcut from check_share_permission; all share
  management now requires being the artifact creator (consistent with
  the removal of superuser bypasses on resource endpoints)
- Add TestDataDictionaryExplicitTenantId, TestRefreshSchemaExplicitTenantId,
  TestTableDetailExplicitTenantId covering 200/403/404 cases for the
  projects API views (the only views using workspace.tenant lazy-load)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct test fixtures so permission checks are actually exercised

- test_artifact_query_data: give other_user their own workspace membership
  in a different tenant so resolve_workspace succeeds for their request;
  artifact lookup then returns 404 (not in their workspace), which is the
  correct behavior to test
- test_share_api: create other_membership and regular_membership in the
  same tenant so resolve_workspace and get_artifact both succeed; only
  then does check_share_permission run and return 403 for non-creators

Previously both test suites passed vacuously because the users had no
memberships of their own and resolve_workspace rejected them immediately
before any creator/membership check was reached.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: return HttpResponse from ArtifactSandboxView on workspace error

ArtifactSandboxView is loaded as an iframe src, so returning a
JsonResponse on workspace resolution failure was incorrect. Return
a plain HttpResponse("Access denied", status=403) instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: send invitation emails asynchronously to prevent HTTP timeout

Both invite and resend handlers were calling send-invitation-email!
synchronously before returning the HTTP response. If SMTP is slow
or misconfigured, this blocks the request thread until socket
timeout (~30-60s), causing the frontend 8000ms XHR to time out.

Wrapping in (future ...) fires the email in the background;
the response returns immediately. The try/catch is preserved
for logging failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(activity-monitor): exponential backoff + process signal acceleration (#177) (#241)

* feat(activity-monitor): exponential backoff + process signal acceleration (#177)

Replace linear backoff with exponential: min(3600, 60 × 5^(n-1))
giving 1m → 5m → 25m → 60m cap. Remove max restart failures limit
(infinite retries). Degrade to DOWN after 1 hour of continuous failure.
Add process signal acceleration: when claudeRunning transitions
false→true, wait 30s grace period then immediately verify via heartbeat,
bypassing remaining backoff.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: self-review fixes for heartbeat engine

P1: Signal acceleration now works in DOWN state too (per design spec).
P2: recoveringStartedAt initialized when resuming in recovering state,
    so DOWN degradation timer works correctly after PM2 restart.
P3: Consolidate multiple Date.now() calls in triggerRecovery.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: auto-detect CLI tool from active provider via providerTools map

Patch 10B v3: resolve CLI tool per-provider instead of global _cfg.tool.
Parse providerId from compound modelId → lookup in _cfg.providerTools →
spawn matched tool or fallback to augmentCLI.

- patches.js: new _buildPatch10BReplace() with providerTools resolve;
  old v2 function renamed to _buildPatch10BV2Replace() for upgrade path
- cli.js: defaultCfg uses providerTools map (claude-code→claude,
  codex→codex); migration converts old tool/args to per-provider entries
- verify.js: structural check for _cfg.providerTools + _pt[_providerId]

VU-417: add genai-toolbox tools.yaml, Dockerfile, and update layer-1 doc (#35)

* VU-417: add genai-toolbox tools.yaml, Dockerfile, and update layer-1 doc

- orchestrator/mssql_mcp/tools.yaml — canonical genai-toolbox config with
  ${ENV_NAME} placeholders; single source of truth for local (Layer 1) and
  GHCR image (Layer 3)
- orchestrator/mssql_mcp/Dockerfile — thin GHCR wrapper image
- docs: update layer-1 doc to reference repo tools.yaml; add MSSQL_HOST/PORT
  to Claude Code MCP env config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: fix toolbox --tools-file flag, use project-scoped .mcp.json, gitignore it

- --config renamed to --tools-file in recent genai-toolbox releases
- MCP server config moved from user ~/.claude.json to project .mcp.json
- Add .mcp.json to .gitignore (contains SA_PASSWORD secret)
- Remove duplicate "Restart Claude Code" line

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: clarify how to invoke mssql-execute-sql from Claude Code

- Add explicit /mcp check step and example prompt format in Step 4
- Update troubleshooting row to reference /mcp command

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: use absolute path for tools-file, add startup verification step

- Relative path unreliable when Claude Code doesn't run from repo root
- Add pre-flight: run toolbox --stdio manually to verify it starts
- Add "configured but not connected" troubleshooting row

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: revert to relative path for tools-file in .mcp.json

Relative path works fine — Claude Code runs from repo root.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: commit .mcp.json with SA_PASSWORD redacted, remove from gitignore

SA_PASSWORD is left blank — fill in locally then run:
  git update-index --skip-worktree .mcp.json

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove genai-toolbox Dockerfile

Switching to stdio subprocess approach for GH Actions (Layer 3).
toolbox binary runs inside the agent step after checkout, pointing at
orchestrator/mssql_mcp/tools.yaml directly. No custom image needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: remove Dockerfile reference from layer-1 doc

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add pre-commit hook to block SA_PASSWORD in .mcp.json

Fails commit if SA_PASSWORD is non-empty in the staged .mcp.json.
Activate per clone/worktree with: git config core.hooksPath .githooks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct tools.yaml schema to match genai-toolbox map format

The list-with-type format was incorrect and did not initialize tools.
Correct format uses:
- sources as a named map with kind: mssql
- tools as a named map with kind: mssql-sql and {{.sql}} template parameter

This is the schema that was validated working in Layer 1 testing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Split Phase 1 into Phase 1A (MVP Go-Live, 8 weeks) and Phase 1B (Complete, 4 weeks)

Phase 1A (16 items): Auth, User Mgmt, RBAC, Products, Categories, Pricing,
Country/Insurer Config, Landing Page, 6-step Quote Wizard, Broker Workflow,
Security & DB. Includes QA, UAT, and regression in 8-week sprint.

Phase 1B (8 items): Dashboard, Partner User Mgmt, Bundle Mgmt, Dependency
Rules, Dependency enforcement, Quote Details, Portal Admin, Partner Portal.

Updated both PPTX and PDF presentation slides 12-13 with 4-phase timeline,
4-column phase breakdown table, and updated item count badges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: disable cache for program_day — always load fresh from DB

At small scale (tens of students), the DB cost is negligible.
Cache was causing stale question order/content after teacher edits
in the web admin. Direct DB queries ensure changes are visible instantly.

perf: optimize pending liquidations endpoint with batched queries

Rewrote /api/gestion/reservations/pending to use 2 batched parallel
queries instead of ~6 sequential queries per owner. Also adds a notice
for pending reservations from other months.
